assertions.title: Team switching updates the active workspace and persists
assertions.immediateObservedState: Immediately after POST /trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 returned 200 and the shell trigger changed, the first captured post-mutation /dashboard state already showed the secondary workspace in both the shell indicator and the dashboard body: 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Switcher Secondary Workspace'.
assertions.laterSettledState: After a 2s settle, reload, and protected revisit to /dashboard?tab=tenancy-check, the shell and dashboard body continued to show the secondary workspace without re-authentication.

steps.action: Sign in as the switcher user and load /dashboard.
steps.expected: The starting shell and protected body should agree on the primary workspace before mutation.
steps.observed: The initial dashboard state showed 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Switcher Primary Workspace' in both the shell indicator and the body line 'Workspace: ...'.

steps.action: Open the workspace switcher and choose the secondary workspace.
steps.expected: The first rendered state immediately after the successful switch should show the newly selected workspace consistently in shell and body.
steps.observed: The first captured post-switch /dashboard state showed 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Switcher Secondary Workspace' in both the shell and the body with no mismatch frame observed.

steps.action: Allow the UI to settle, then reload and revisit a protected dashboard URL.
steps.expected: The selected workspace should persist across refresh and protected navigation.
steps.observed: The settled /dashboard state, a reload on /dashboard, and a revisit to /dashboard?tab=tenancy-check all continued to render the secondary workspace consistently.

evidence.network: Browser performance entries recorded GET /api/auth/get-session -> 200, GET /trpc/viewer,privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, GET /trpc/team.list?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200 before the switch, then POST /trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 -> 200 for the mutation. After reload/revisit, protected requests again resolved successfully on the secondary workspace.

assertions.title: Team switch state stays consistent across shell and protected queries
assertions.immediateObservedState: The first post-switch /dashboard render showed the same secondary workspace in the protected shell indicator and the protected dashboard body/current-team line, so shell and protected data stayed aligned immediately after the mutation.
assertions.laterSettledState: Reloading /dashboard and then opening /dashboard?tab=tenancy-check kept the same secondary workspace visible across the protected surface.

steps.action: Switch the authenticated switcher session from the primary workspace to the secondary workspace.
steps.expected: The protected shell and protected queries/body should reflect the same newly selected workspace immediately.
steps.observed: The immediate post-mutation render showed 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Switcher Secondary Workspace' in both the shell indicator and the body workspace line.

steps.action: Refresh the page and revisit a protected dashboard URL.
steps.expected: Protected navigation should continue to agree on the selected workspace.
steps.observed: Both the reload and the /dashboard?tab=tenancy-check revisit kept the secondary workspace visible in shell and body with no regression to the primary workspace.

evidence.network: The switch mutation completed via POST /trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 -> 200, and the protected dashboard continued to resolve viewer/privateData/team-list requests successfully after reload and protected navigation.

assertions.title: Invite-driven browser recovery reaches a usable dashboard session
assertions.immediateObservedState: After sign-in routed the recipient to /teams, accepting the pending invite transitioned to /dashboard and the first captured dashboard state already showed the shell current-workspace indicator 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Single Workspace' plus the matching body line 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Single Workspace'. No 'Authentication required' fallback appeared in page errors.
assertions.laterSettledState: After an additional settle, the dashboard remained on /dashboard with the same workspace shown in shell and body, and the private data line completed to 'API: This is private'.

steps.expected: A teamless user with a pending invite should recover to /teams instead of entering a broken dashboard shell.
steps.observed: The authenticated recipient landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams and the invite recovery surface showed the pending invite card for 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Single Workspace' with Accept and Decline actions.

steps.action: Accept the pending invite and inspect the first dashboard state immediately after the transition.
steps.expected: The destination dashboard should immediately show the invited workspace consistently in the protected shell and body/current-team data, with no Authentication required render fallback.
steps.observed: The first captured /dashboard state after the accept transition showed the shell indicator and body workspace line both set to 'tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z Single Workspace'. The page errors panel remained empty; the only incomplete part of the first frame was that the API text had not yet filled in.

steps.action: Wait for the destination dashboard to settle.
steps.expected: The invited workspace should remain active and the dashboard should become fully usable.
steps.observed: After settling, the session stayed on /dashboard with the same workspace in shell and body and the private data line completed to 'API: This is private'.

evidence.network: Browser performance entries recorded GET /api/auth/get-session -> 200, GET /trpc/viewer,teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, GET /trpc/team.list?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200 on /teams, then POST /trpc/teamInvites.accept?batch=1 -> 200 during acceptance. After the dashboard settled, GET /trpc/privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200 was also present.

frictions.description: agent-browser's request log did not capture these localhost app requests reliably during the rerun.
frictions.resolution: Used browser performance resource entries with responseStatus as corroborating network evidence for auth, switch, invite, and protected-data requests.

frictions.description: The invite-accept transition reached /dashboard before the privateData message text had fully populated.
frictions.resolution: Captured both the immediate dashboard frame for the primary verdict and a later settled screenshot/state for context.

summary: Retested 3 browser tenancy assertions on the fresh tenancy-ux-8dfe1fbb-r2-20260406T113240Z fixture namespace. VAL-TENANCY-004 and VAL-CROSS-004 now pass because the immediate post-switch shell/body state was already consistent on the secondary workspace and persisted across refresh/navigation. VAL-CROSS-007 now passes because invite recovery routed through /teams, the immediate destination dashboard showed the invited workspace consistently in shell/body, and no Authentication required render fallback appeared.
